mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad economic strength and robust corporate earnings growth, leading to positive equity market returns. This is a direct, always-on measure of economic momentum.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year change in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3%, it signals persistent inflation that erodes corporate profit margins and prompts restrictive monetary policy, causing negative forward returns for equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Elevated inflation often precedes Fed tightening cycles.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market, signaled by the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, indicates a recessionary shock, leading to a sharp decline in equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:The 'Sahm Rule' uses a similar UNRATE trigger as a recession indicator.
failure_condition: The median 60-day forward return following a trigger event is not statistically significantly negative (p > 0.05) over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in retail sales (RSAFS) and a rise in unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a collapse in consumer demand and confidence. This joint signal of weakening consumption and labor markets precedes negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the combined signal is not significantly greater than the IC of either individual proxy series over a 5-year lookback.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) signals that supply chains are normalizing and output is expanding to meet recovering demand. This is a strong positive impulse for corporate revenues and equity prices.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE of 7.5% and FEDFUNDS of 0.15% are consistent with a recovery phase.
failure_condition: The signal's positive correlation with forward returns disappears or reverses when the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained positive trend in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of broad economic activity, reflecting consumer confidence and driving growth in multiple sectors. This leads to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 6-month change in HOUST and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive over the 2000-2010 period.

mechanism_narrative: The absolute level of the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) acts as a valuation anchor. When the rate is above 4%, it creates a high hurdle rate for equities and offers a competitive risk-free alternative, depressing S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The equity risk premium is compressed when risk-free rates are high.
failure_condition: Average 60-day S&P 500 returns are not statistically lower when FEDFUNDS is above 4% compared to when it is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A stagflationary environment, identified by high inflation (rising CPIAUCSL) and slowing real growth (falling GDPC1), is exceptionally negative for equities. This interaction compresses profit margins and reduces real earnings, leading to poor returns.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits positive average 60-day returns during periods where the trigger condition is met for at least two consecutive quarters.
